programs.name: Community Leadership After-School Program (CLASP)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? Community Leadership After School Program (CLASP) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Certification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference offers a great opportunity to be introduced to new techniques and learn about emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops.The Shallow/Deep Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification provides the opportunity to complete the theoretical portion of becoming a certified Aquatic Fitness Instructor. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Recertification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference introduces new techniques and explores emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops. The conference provides the opportunity for instructors to recertify their WaterArt qualifications through the two day workshops.Additionally, there is the opportunity to add a specialty certification to your resume. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of taking a specialty certification but is not a requirement for those wishing to attend the conference only.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification
programs.description: The Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification course is offered by WaterART Fitness International. This certification provides instructors with the  skills they require to teach an engaging and safe aquatic fitness class in both shallow and deep water. These skills include class management, water movements, understanding major muscle groups, joint action, and how to design target training sets. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will also be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard Frist Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor: Specialty - Mind and Body
programs.description: Combine the calm of an aquatic environment with mindful movements. Focusing on balance and breath, elements from Pilates, tai chi and yoga will be incorporated into this workout.

programs.name: Aquatic Supervisor
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. For deck-level supervisory staff, the Aquatic Supervisor course provides the knowledge and skills to manage a safe aquatic environment.Prerequisite: National Lifeguard or Lifesaving Instructor or Swim Instructor certification (current or expired). For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Basic First Aid CPR-C & AED
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24) . Candidates will learn first-aid principles and the emergency treatment of injuries. This course teaches victim assessment, CPR, choking and how to respond to external bleeding, heart attack, stroke, wounds and burns.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.

Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Bronze Star
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.

programs.name: Examiner Course
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course is the first step in a three-step process to become certified as an Examiner for the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course prepares candidates to apprentice successfully as an Examiner on the exam of their choice, and it builds on the evaluation experience that candidates have attained in instructor evaluated awards and content.Prerequisite: Current instructor certification for the stream they are seeking examiner certification and being an experienced instructor, having taught or co-taught at least one course in that stream.Equipment: Bring the Award Guide(s) for the streams you wish to become an examiner in.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Instructor Recert Course
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This in-person recertification course focuses on the competencies learned in the instructor, coach and examiner courses and provides a refresher to those competencies. Course content will be updated every two years. This course provides the 4 points required to complete the leadership recertification for instructors, coaches, examiners and examiner mentors. This course does not automatically recertify a candidate. Following successful course completion candidates must complete the recertification process to be recertified.Prerequisite: Current Swim Instructor and/or Lifesaving Instructor and/or Examiners and/or any Coach Certification. Candidates must bring their instructor course materials to this recert course. These may include: Swim For Life Award Guide or Toronto Learn to Swim Manual, Instructor Manual, Swim Patrol Award Guide, Bronze Medals Award Guide, First Aid Award Guide, Teaching Swim for Life.Note: This course can be completed at any time during your 24 month currency period and leadership recertification online form submitted with payment when your awards are expiring.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Examiner Course
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course is the first step in a three-step process to become certified as an Examiner for the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course prepares candidates to apprentice successfully as an Examiner on the exam of their choice, and it builds on the evaluation experience that candidates have attained in instructor evaluated awards and content.Prerequisite: Current instructor certification for the stream they are seeking examiner certification and being an experienced instructor, having taught or co-taught at least one course in that stream.Equipment: Bring the Award Guide(s) for the streams you wish to become an examiner in.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Instructor Recert Course
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This in-person recertification course focuses on the competencies learned in the instructor, coach and examiner courses and provides a refresher to those competencies. Course content will be updated every two years. This course provides the 4 points required to complete the leadership recertification for instructors, coaches, examiners and examiner mentors. This course does not automatically recertify a candidate. Following successful course completion candidates must complete the recertification process to be recertified.Prerequisite: Current Swim Instructor and/or Lifesaving Instructor and/or Examiners and/or any Coach Certification. Candidates must bring their instructor course materials to this recert course. These may include: Swim For Life Award Guide or Toronto Learn to Swim Manual, Instructor Manual, Swim Patrol Award Guide, Bronze Medals Award Guide, First Aid Award Guide, Teaching Swim for Life.Note: This course can be completed at any time during your 24 month currency period and leadership recertification online form submitted with payment when your awards are expiring.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24). This recertification course provides a review of first aid and CPR skills. programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification or Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification or Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification or Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification or Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This recertification course offers candidates with certification that will be expiring the opportunity to maintain current certification at the National Lifeguard level in a pool setting. Your lifeguarding skills will be tested based on physical standards, judgment and skill.Prerequisite: National Lifeguard - Pool (current or expired).Equipment: Bring a whistle.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Swim and Lifesaving Instructor
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Participants learn to organize, plan, teach and evaluate swimming, lifesaving and first aid skills. Discover and develop skills in the areas of class management, teaching, evaluation, leadership, communication and administration. With the successful completion of this course, candidates will be qualified to instruct the Toronto Learn to Swim program, the Lifesaving Society Swim for Life program, Emergency First Aid with CPR-B, Rookie, Ranger and Star Patrol as well as Bronze Star, Medallion and Cross.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old and Bronze Cross (current or expired).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.
